devanagari: गर्ग आगमनं जातककथनपूर्वकं यशोदारोहिणीसुतयोः । नामकरणसंस्कारः मृद्भक्षणव्याजेन यशोदायै । विश्वरूपप्रकटीकरणंच । श्रीशुक उवाच । गर्गः पुरोह...
english_devnagari: garga āgamanaṃ jātakakathanapūrvakaṃ yaśodārohiṇīsutayoḥ nāmakaraṇasaṃskāraḥ mṛdbhakṣaṇavyājena yaśodāyai viśvarūpaprakaṭīkaraṇaṃca śrīśuka uvāca gargaḥ purohito rājan yadūnāṃ sumahātapāḥ vrajaṃ jagāma nandasya vasudeva pracoditaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
At the request of Vasudeva, Garga, the family priest of Yadus, noted for rigid asceticism, visited Nanda’s Vraja, Oh king.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा परमप्रीतः प्रत्युत्थाय कृताञ्जलिः । आनर्चाधोक्षजधिया प्रणिपातपुरःसरम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā paramaprītaḥ pratyutthāya kṛtāñjaliḥ ānarcādhokṣajadhiyā praṇipātapuraḥsaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He (Nanda) was highly pleased to see Garga. He stood up to show respect to him and folded his palms. He worshipped him, lying prostrate before him, regarded him to be Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: सूपविष्टं कृतातिथ्यं गिरा सूनृतया मुनिम् । नन्दयित्वाब्रवीद् ब्रह्मन् पूर्णस्य करवाम किम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sūpaviṣṭaṃ kṛtātithyaṃ girā sūnṛtayā munim nandayitvābravīd brahman pūrṇasya karavāma kim

translation: When the sage was properly received and comfortably seated, Nanda, pleasing him with sweet words, asked, “Oh Brāhmaṇa, what service should I render to you who are selfaccomplished (and want nothing).

devanagari: महद्विचलनं नॄणां गृहिणां दीनचेतसाम् । निःश्रेयसाय भगवन् कल्पते नान्यथा क्वचित् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: mahadvicalanaṃ nṝṇāṃ gṛhiṇāṃ dīnacetasām niḥśreyasāya bhagavan kalpate nānyathā kvacit
translation: Oh venerable sage I Visits of great people like you contribute to the supreme good to persons who are attached to their households and are distressed in their minds. They never turn out to the contrary.

devanagari: ज्योतिषामयनं साक्षाद् यत्तज्ज्ञानं अतीन्द्रियम् । प्रणीतं भवता येन पुमान्वेद परावरम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: jyotiṣāmayanaṃ sākṣād yattajjñānaṃ atīndriyam praṇītaṃ bhavatā yena pumānveda parāvaram

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: You have directly compiled a treatise on the science of astrology (lit. the movements of luminaries) which is beyond the range of senses. It is by that that a man knows his past and future.

devanagari: त्वं हि ब्रह्मविदां श्रेष्ठः संस्कारान् कर्तुमर्हसि । बालयोरनयोर्नॄणां जन्मना ब्राह्मणो गुरुः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ hi brahmavidāṃ śreṣṭhaḥ saṃskārān kartumarhasi bālayoranayornṝṇāṃ janmanā brāhmaṇo guruḥ
translation: You are foremost among the knowers of the Vedas. You should, therefore, perform the purificatory rites of these two boys; for a Brāhmaṇa is the preceptor of all people, by his very birth.”

devanagari: श्रीगर्ग उवाच । यदूनां अहमाचार्यः ख्यातश्च भुवि सर्वतः । सुतं मया संस्कृतं ते मन्यते देवकीसुतम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīgarga uvāca yadūnāṃ ahamācāryaḥ khyātaśca bhuvi sarvataḥ sutaṃ mayā saṃskṛtaṃ te manyate devakīsutam

translation:
Garga said:
“I am the family priest of the Yaḍus and I am known over the world as such. If I were to consecrate your son with religious rites, they will regard him as the son of Devakī.

devanagari: कंसः पापमतिः सख्यं तव चानकदुन्दुभेः । देवक्या अष्टमो गर्भो न स्त्री भवितुमर्हति ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsaḥ pāpamatiḥ sakhyaṃ tava cānakadundubheḥ devakyā aṣṭamo garbho na strī bhavitumarhati
translation: Kaṃsa is wicked by nature. The friendship between you and Vasudeva (is also well known). Kaṃsa has heard the words of Devakī’s daughter that the eighth born (womb) of Devakī cannot be a female. If taking into account all these, he (Kaṃa) gets suspicions, and if he were to kill (your son), it will be our blunder.”

devanagari: इति सञ्चिन्तयन् श्रुत्वा देवक्या दारिकावचः । अपि हन्ता आगताशङ्कः तर्हि तन्नोऽनयो भवेत् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sañcintayan śrutvā devakyā dārikāvacaḥ api hantā āgatāśaṅkaḥ tarhi tanno

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Kaṃsa is wicked by nature. The friendship between you and Vasudeva (is also well known). Kaṃsa has heard the words of Devakī’s daughter that the eighth born (womb) of Devakī cannot be a female. If taking into account all these, he (Kaṃa) gets suspicions, and if he were to kill (your son), it will be our blunder.”

devanagari: श्रीनन्द उवाच । अलक्षितोऽस्मिन् रहसि मामकैरपि गोव्रजे । कुरु द्विजातिसंस्कारं स्वस्तिवाचनपूर्वकम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnanda uvāca alakṣito kuru dvijātisaṃskāraṃ svastivācanapūrvakam

translation:
Nanda replied:
“Please perform the purificatory rites proper for members of the twice-born castes, invoking heavenly blessings with benedictory hymns. Please do so secretly at Gokula, without being noticed even by my own people.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं संप्रार्थितो विप्रः स्वचिकीर्षितमेव तत् । चकार नामकरणं गूढो रहसि बालयोः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ saṃprārthito vipraḥ svacikīrṣitameva tat cakāra nāmakaraṇaṃ gūḍho rahasi bālayoḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus requested (by Nanda), the Brāhmaṇa who himself was desirous of it performed the naming ceremony of the two boys (the sons of Rohiṇī and Yaśodā) secretly, remaining himself unnoticed.

devanagari: श्रीगर्ग उवाच । अयं हि रोहिणीपुत्रो रमयन् सुहृदो गुणैः । आख्यास्यते राम इति बलाधिक्याद् बलं विदुः । यदूनामपृथग्भावात् सङ्कर्षणमुशन्त्यु...
english_devnagari: śrīgarga uvāca ayaṃ hi rohiṇīputro ramayan suhṛdo guṇaiḥ ākhyāsyate rāma iti balādhikyād balaṃ viduḥ yadūnāmapṛthagbhāvāt saṅkarṣaṇamuśantyuta

translation:
Garga said:
“This son of Rohiṇī will be called Rāma as he will delight his friends with his amiable excellent qualities, and he will be known as Bala due to his superiority in strength. They will call him Saṅkarṣaṇa due to his capacity to draw together and unite dissenting Yadus (Garga does not want to disclose the transfer of Balarāma from the womb of Devakī to Rohiṇī due to which he was really called Saṅkarṣaṇa).

devanagari: आसन् वर्णास्त्रयो ह्यस्य गृह्णतोऽनुयुगं तनूः । शुक्लो रक्तस्तथा पीत इदानीं कृष्णतां गतः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: āsan varṇāstrayo hyasya gṛhṇato śuklo raktastathā pīta idānīṃ kṛṣṇatāṃ gataḥ

translation: As to this (second son), he assumed human forms in every Yuga with three different complexions, viz., white, red and yellow for each, successively. Now he has assumed the dark complexion. (Hence he will be known as Kṛṣṇa).

devanagari: प्रागयं वसुदेवस्य क्वचित् जातस्तवात्मजः । वासुदेव इति श्रीमान् अभिज्ञाः संप्रचक्षते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: prāgayaṃ vasudevasya kvacit jātastavātmajaḥ vāsudeva iti śrīmān abhijñāḥ saṃpracakṣate

translation: Formerly, this son of yours was sometime born in the house of Vasudeva. Hence the wise (knowers of this fact) will designate him as Vāsudeva, the glorious.

devanagari: बहूनि सन्ति नामानि रूपाणि च सुतस्य ते । गुणकर्मानुरूपाणि तान्यहं वेद नो जनाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bahūni santi nāmāni rūpāṇi ca sutasya te guṇakarmānurūpāṇi tānyahaṃ veda no janāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Numerous are the names and forms of this son of yours suitable to excellent qualities and marvellous deeds. I know them (all), but not the common people.

devanagari: एष वः श्रेय आधास्यद् गोपगोकुलनन्दनः । अनेन सर्वदुर्गाणि यूयं अञ्जस्तरिष्यथ ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa vaḥ śreya ādhāsyad gopagokulanandanaḥ anena sarvadurgāṇi yūyaṃ añjastariṣyatha

translation: This delight of Gopas and Gokula (i.e. Kṛṣṇa) will bring prosperity to you all, and you will easily surmount all calamities with his help.

devanagari: पुरानेन व्रजपते साधवो दस्युपीडिताः । अराजके रक्ष्यमाणा जिग्युर्दस्यून् समेधिताः ॥ १७ ॥

translation: In times of yore, while anarchy prevailed, he protected pious people who were harassed by rogues and robbers, and when strengthened by him, they (righteous people) vanquished the miscreants.

devanagari: य एतस्मिन् महाभागाः प्रीतिं कुर्वन्ति मानवाः । नारयोऽभिभवन्त्येतान् विष्णुपक्षानिवासुराः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etasmin mahābhāgāḥ prītiṃ kurvanti mānavāḥ nārayo

translation: Just as those who side with Viṣṇu are not vanquished by Asuras, those fortunate persons who (passionately) love him are not overpowered by enemies.

devanagari: तस्मात् नन्दात्मजोऽयं ते नारायणसमो गुणैः । श्रिया कीर्त्यानुभावेन गोपायस्व समाहितः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt nandātmajo śriyā kīrtyānubhāvena gopāyasva samāhitaḥ
translation: Therefore, Oh Nanda, this son of yours is a compeer of Lord Nārāyaṇa[1] in excellent qualities, splendour, glory and prowess. Therefore, please take care of him with very minute attention.”
purport:
Footnote [1]:
To whom Nārāyaṇa bears resemblance by his attributes—Kramasandarbha.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यात्मानं समादिश्य गर्गे च स्वगृहं गते । नन्दः प्रमुदितो मेने आत्मानं पूर्णमाशिषाम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityātmānaṃ samādiśya garge ca svagṛhaṃ gate nandaḥ pramudito mene ātmānaṃ pūrṇamāśiṣām

translation: When Garga returned home after instructing Nanda thus (or addressing Lord Kṛṣṇa), Nanda was so highly delighted that he considered himself blessed with all well-beings.

translation: In a short while. Rāma and Keśava began to creep on their hands and knees and sportfully went round Gokula.

devanagari: तावङ्घ्रियुग्ममनुकृष्य सरीसृपन्तौ । घोषप्रघोषरुचिरं व्रजकर्दमेषु । तन्नादहृष्टमनसावनुसृत्य लोकं । मुग्धप्रभीतवदुपेयतुरन्ति मात्रोः ॥ २२...

translation: While they crawled speedily even in the moist places of Vraja, dragging the pair of their legs (behind them), there arose a sweet tinkling sound of the tiny bells of their girdles and anklets. Their minds being delighted with the jingling sound, they followed the passers-by (a few steps) only to return to their mothers, as if in bewilderment and fright.

devanagari: तन्मातरौ निजसुतौ घृणया स्नुवन्त्यौ । पङ्काङ्गरागरुचिरौ उपगृह्य दोर्भ्याम् । दत्त्वा स्तनं प्रपिबतोः स्म मुखं निरीक्ष्य । मुग्धस्मिताल्प...
english_devnagari: tanmātarau nijasutau ghṛṇayā snuvantyau paṅkāṅgarāgarucirau upagṛhya dorbhyām dattvā stanaṃ prapibatoḥ sma mukhaṃ nirīkṣya mugdhasmitālpadaśanaṃ yayatuḥ pramodam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ snu class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ snu class 2 verb]

translation: Their two mothers with their breasts spontaneously overflowing with milk due to motherly affection, embraced in their arms their sons who looked charming with their bodies besmeared with muck They enjoyed ecstatic delight while gazing at their (sons’) countenances beaming with bewitching smiles and tiny tender teeth when they were sucking the breasts.

devanagari: यर्ह्यङ्गनादर्शनीयकुमारलीलौ । अन्तर्व्रजे तदबलाः प्रगृहीतपुच्छैः । वत्सैरितस्तत उभावनुकृष्यमाणौ । प्रेक्षन्त्य उज्झितगृहा जहृषुर्हसन्त्...
english_devnagari: yarhyaṅganādarśanīyakumāralīlau antarvraje tadabalāḥ pragṛhītapucchaiḥ vatsairitastata ubhāvanukṛṣyamāṇau prekṣantya ujjhitagṛhā jahṛṣurhasantyaḥ

[nominative plural from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: When they attained to the age when the sports of boys become attractive to women to see, the damsels of Vraja witnessed how both of them, holding the tails of calves were dragged behind them hither and thither. Forgetting their household duties, they burst out laughing and were transported with joy.

devanagari: शृङ्ग्यग्निदंष्ट्र्यसिजल द्विजकण्टकेभ्यः । क्रीडापरावतिचलौ स्वसुतौ निषेद्धुम् । गृह्याणि कर्तुमपि यत्र न तज्जनन्यौ । शेकात आपतुरलं मनसो...
english_devnagari: śṛṅgyagnidaṃṣṭryasijala dvijakaṇṭakebhyaḥ krīḍāparāvaticalau svasutau niṣeddhum gṛhyāṇi kartumapi yatra na tajjananyau śekāta āpaturalaṃ manaso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: When their mothers found it impossible to restrain their extremely active and playful sons from horned beasts, fire, animals of sharp teeth, sharp lethal weapons, water, (carnivorous, cruel) birds and thorns or even to attend to their household duties, they experienced a great mental (strain of) distraction.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā regards this as the highest point of their domestic happiness.

devanagari: कालेनाल्पेन राजर्षे रामः कृष्णश्च गोकुले । अघृष्टजानुभिः पद्भिः विचक्रमतुरञ्जसा ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kālenālpena rājarṣe rāmaḥ kṛṣṇaśca gokule aghṛṣṭajānubhiḥ padbhiḥ vicakramaturañjasā
translation: After a short time, Oh royal sage, Rāma and Kṛṣṇa began to move about easily on feet, in Gokul, without rubbing their knees (against the floor).

devanagari: ततस्तु भगवान् कृष्णो वयस्यैर्व्रजबालकैः । सहरामो व्रजस्त्रीणां चिक्रीडे जनयन् मुदम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastu bhagavān kṛṣṇo vayasyairvrajabālakaiḥ saharāmo vrajastrīṇāṃ cikrīḍe janayan mudam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ], [vocative single from √ jan ]

[nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ], [vocative single from √ jan ], [accusative single from √ jan ]

translation: Then, in course of time, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa accompanied by Balarāma began to play with boys of his own age, enhancing the joys of the women of Vraja.

devanagari: कृष्णस्य गोप्यो रुचिरं वीक्ष्य कौमारचापलम् । श्रृण्वन्त्याः किल तन्मातुः इति होचुः समागताः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasya gopyo ruciraṃ vīkṣya kaumāracāpalam śrṛṇvantyāḥ kila tanmātuḥ iti hocuḥ samāgatāḥ
translation: Observing the charming boyish pranks of Kṛṣṇa, the cowherd-women came in a body (to his house) and actually spoke (complained) as follows within the hearing of his mother.

devanagari: वत्सान् मुञ्चन् क्वचिदसमये क्रोशसञ्जातहासः । स्तेयं स्वाद्वत्त्यथ दधिपयः कल्पितैः स्तेययोगैः । मर्कान् भोक्ष्यन् विभजति स चेन्नात्ति भा...
english_devnagari: vatsān muñcan kvacidasamaye krośasañjātahāsaḥ steyaṃ svādvattyatha dadhipayaḥ kalpitaiḥ steyayogaiḥ markān bhokṣyan vibhajati sa cennātti bhāṇḍaṃ bhinnatti dravyālābhe sagṛhakupito yātyupakrośya tokān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muc class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ muñc class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ muñc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ stai class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ stai class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: “Sometimes he untethers the calves untimely (before the milking time of cows allowing the calves to drink up all the milk). If scolded, he (derisively) laughs. Inventing novel ways of stealing, he drinks up the sweet and tasty curds and milk stolen by him. Sometimes he distributes it to the monkeys. If the monkey does not partake of it, he smashes the jar (or milk or curds). If he gets no material (to eat), he gets angry with the infants (people in the) house and goes away setting the infants (in the house) crying.

devanagari: हस्ताग्राह्ये रचयति विधिं पीठकोलूखलाद्यैः । छिद्रं ह्यन्तर्निहितवयुनः शिक्यभाण्डेषु तद्वित् । ध्वान्तागारे धृतमणिगणं स्वाङ्गमर्थप्रदीपं...
english_devnagari: hastāgrāhye racayati vidhiṃ pīṭhakolūkhalādyaiḥ chidraṃ hyantarnihitavayunaḥ śikyabhāṇḍeṣu tadvit dhvāntāgāre dhṛtamaṇigaṇaṃ svāṅgamarthapradīpaṃ kāle gopyo yarhi gṛhakṛtyeṣu suvyagracittāḥ
translation: If the jar (of milk, curds or butter) is (kept hanging) beyond the reach of his hands, he constructs contrivance with the help of wooden seats and mortars. Knowing the contents of the pots suspended in slings, he strikes holes into the pots. In a dark room he uses like a lamp his own person, decorated profusely with jewels—he does all this at a time when the Gopa women have their minds fully engrossed in performing their household duties.

devanagari: एवं धार्ष्ट्यान्युशति कुरुते मेहनादीनि वास्तौ । स्तेयोपायैर्विरचितकृतिः सुप्रतीको यथास्ते । इत्थं स्त्रीभिः सभयनयन श्रीमुखालोकिनीभिः । ...
english_devnagari: evaṃ dhārṣṭyānyuśati kurute mehanādīni vāstau steyopāyairviracitakṛtiḥ supratīko yathāste itthaṃ strībhiḥ sabhayanayana śrīmukhālokinībhiḥ vyākhyātārthā prahasitamukhī na hyupālabdhumaicchat
translation:
In this way, he commits acts of nuisance such as passing urine and leaving excretions in our cleansed houses. Having perpetrated such mischiefs and using all devices of stealing, he sits near you like an innocent boy of good behaviour”.
Even though such complaints were lodged by women who gazed at his beautiful face with terrified eyes, Yaśodā full of smiles did not feel inclined to chastise him.

devanagari: एकदा क्रीडमानास्ते रामाद्या गोपदारकाः । कृष्णो मृदं भक्षितवान् इति मात्रे न्यवेदयन् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā krīḍamānāste rāmādyā gopadārakāḥ kṛṣṇo mṛdaṃ bhakṣitavān iti mātre nyavedayan
translation: On one occasion, while cowherd-boys headed by Balarāma were playing, they complained to the mother (Yaśodā) that Kṛṣṇa had eaten earth.

devanagari: सा गृहीत्वा करे कृष्णं उपालभ्य हितैषिणी । यशोदा भयसंभ्रान्त प्रेक्षणाक्षमभाषत ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā gṛhītvā kare kṛṣṇaṃ upālabhya hitaiṣiṇī yaśodā bhayasaṃbhrānta prekṣaṇākṣamabhāṣata
translation: Thereupon, Yaśodā who was anxious about his wellbeing and health), caught Kṛṣṇa by his hand and rebuking him, while his eyes assumed bewildering look (as if) through fear, she spoke:

devanagari: कस्मान् मृदमदान्तात्मन् भवान् भक्षितवान् रहः । वदन्ति तावका ह्येते कुमारास्तेऽग्रजोऽप्ययम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kasmān mṛdamadāntātman bhavān bhakṣitavān rahaḥ vadanti tāvakā hyete kumārāste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: ‘You naughty boy! Why did you eat earth secretly? These your playmates tell me and this your elder brother also says the same.’

devanagari: नाहं भक्षितवान् अंब सर्वे मिथ्याभिशंसिनः । यदि सत्यगिरस्तर्हि समक्षं पश्य मे मुखम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ bhakṣitavān aṃba sarve mithyābhiśaṃsinaḥ yadi satyagirastarhi samakṣaṃ paśya me mukham
translation: ‘No mother, I did not eat earth. All these are liars. If (you still think that) they tell the truth, you look into my mouth personally.’

devanagari: यद्येवं तर्हि व्यादेही इत्युक्तः स भगवान् हरिः । व्यादत्ताव्याहतैश्वर्यः क्रीडामनुजबालकः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyevaṃ tarhi vyādehī ityuktaḥ sa bhagavān hariḥ vyādattāvyāhataiśvaryaḥ krīḍāmanujabālakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: ‘If that be the case, open your mouth.’ Thus ordered, the glorious Lord Hari of unlimited supreme powers, and who, out of sport, had assumed the form of a human child, opened his mouth.

devanagari: सा तत्र ददृशे विश्वं जगत् स्थास्नु च खं दिशः । साद्रिद्वीपाब्धिभूगोलं सवाय्वग्नीन्दुतारकम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tatra dadṛśe viśvaṃ jagat sthāsnu ca khaṃ diśaḥ sādridvīpābdhibhūgolaṃ savāyvagnīndutārakam
translation: She (Yaśodā) beheld therein the universe consisting of mobiles and immobiles, the ethereal dome, the cardinal points, the sphere of the earth along with the mountains, continents and oceans, the wind, the fire (of lightning), the moon, the stars, the (whole of the) planetary system, (elements like) water, fire, wind and the sky, (the presiding deities of) the senses and the mind, subtle elements and the three guṇas.

devanagari: ज्योतिश्चक्रं जलं तेजो नभस्वान् वियदेव च । वैकारिकाणीन्द्रियाणि मनो मात्रा गुणास्त्रयः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: jyotiścakraṃ jalaṃ tejo nabhasvān viyadeva ca vaikārikāṇīndriyāṇi mano mātrā guṇāstrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vī class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vī class 2 verb]

translation: She (Yaśodā) beheld therein the universe consisting of mobiles and immobiles, the ethereal dome, the cardinal points, the sphere of the earth along with the mountains, continents and oceans, the wind, the fire (of lightning), the moon, the stars, the (whole of the) planetary system, (elements like) water, fire, wind and the sky, (the presiding deities of) the senses and the mind, subtle elements and the three guṇas.

devanagari: एतद्विचित्रं सहजीवकाल । स्वभावकर्माशयलिङ्गभेदम् । सूनोस्तनौ वीक्ष्य विदारितास्ये । व्रजं सहात्मानमवाप शङ्काम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: etadvicitraṃ sahajīvakāla svabhāvakarmāśayaliṅgabhedam sūnostanau vīkṣya vidāritāsye vrajaṃ sahātmānamavāpa śaṅkām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Seeing in the body, nay in the gaping mouth of her child, this marvellous universe consisting of a variety of organisms produced by (factors like Souls, Time, Nature, Destiny and Mind as well as the whole of Vraja including herself, she was seized with terror.

devanagari: किं स्वप्न एतदुत देवमाया । किं वा मदीयो बत बुद्धिमोहः । अथो अमुष्यैव ममार्भकस्य । यः कश्चनौत्पत्तिक आत्मयोगः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ svapna etaduta devamāyā kiṃ vā madīyo bata buddhimohaḥ atho amuṣyaiva mamārbhakasya yaḥ kaścanautpattika ātmayogaḥ
translation: (She bemused) “Is this a dream or an illusion created by the Lord? Or is it a fantasy—a delusion of my own mind? Or is it the innate divine power of this very child of mine?

devanagari: अथो यथावन्न वितर्कगोचरं । चेतोमनःकर्मवचोभिरञ्जसा । यदाश्रयं येन यतः प्रतीयते । सुदुर्विभाव्यं प्रणतास्मि तत्पदम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: atho yathāvanna vitarkagocaraṃ cetomanaḥkarmavacobhirañjasā yadāśrayaṃ yena yataḥ pratīyate sudurvibhāvyaṃ praṇatāsmi tatpadam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ bhū ], [accusative single from √ bhū ]

translation: Hence I bow down to the feet of the Supreme Lord, which are extremely difficult to comprehend and the real nature of which cannot be grasped easily by the intellect, mind, deeds and words; which are the basis of the universe and through the instrumentality of which and from out of which the universe is manifested.

devanagari: अहं ममासौ पतिरेष मे सुतो । व्रजेश्वरस्याखिलवित्तपा सती । गोप्यश्च गोपाः सहगोधनाश्च मे । यन्माययेत्थं कुमतिः स मे गतिः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ mamāsau patireṣa me suto vrajeśvarasyākhilavittapā satī gopyaśca gopāḥ sahagodhanāśca me yanmāyayetthaṃ kumatiḥ sa me gatiḥ

translation: He (that Supreme Lord) is my refuge by whose deluding potency (Māyā) arise perverse notions such as ‘I am (Yaśodā)’, ‘This is my husband’, ‘This (Kṛṣṇa) is my son’, ‘I am that chaste lady who protect the whole property of Nanda (the Lord of Vraja) ‘All the cowherds—men and women along with the wealth of cattle—are mine’.”

devanagari: इत्थं विदित तत्त्वायां गोपिकायां स ईश्वरः । वैष्णवीं व्यतनोन्मायां पुत्रस्नेहमयीं विभुः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ vidita tattvāyāṃ gopikāyāṃ sa īśvaraḥ vaiṣṇavīṃ vyatanonmāyāṃ putrasnehamayīṃ vibhuḥ
translation: The Omnipotent Supreme Lord cast over the Gopa- lady who had thus realized the truth, his deluding Vaiṣṇava (divine) charm which filled her with maternal affection for (her) son.

devanagari: सद्यो नष्टस्मृतिर्गोपी साऽरोप्यारोहमात्मजम् । प्रवृद्धस्नेहकलिल हृदयासीद् यथा पुरा ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sadyo naṣṭasmṛtirgopī sā pravṛddhasnehakalila hṛdayāsīd yathā purā

translation: Instantly the Gopa-lady lost her memory (regarding the real nature of the Lord). She placed her son on her lap. Her heart overflowed with intense affection as before.

devanagari: त्रय्या चोपनिषद्भिश्च साङ्ख्ययोगैश्च सात्वतैः । उपगीयमान माहात्म्यं हरिं सामन्यतात्मजम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: trayyā copaniṣadbhiśca sāṅkhyayogaiśca sātvataiḥ upagīyamāna māhātmyaṃ hariṃ sāmanyatātmajam

translation: She began to look upon Hari as her own son—Hari whose glory is being sung by the three Vedas, Upaniṣads, Sāṅkhya and Yoga systems and by Sātvatas (or votaries of the Lord).

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । नन्दः किमकरोद् ब्रह्मन् श्रेय एवं महोदयम् । यशोदा च महाभागा पपौ यस्याः स्तनं हरिः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca nandaḥ kimakarod brahman śreya evaṃ mahodayam yaśodā ca mahābhāgā papau yasyāḥ stanaṃ hariḥ

translation:
The King said:
Oh Brāhmaṇa Sage! What highly meritorious act did Nanda perform so as to deserve such great fortune? What did the highly fortunate Yośodā do that Lord Hari sucked her breasts?

devanagari: पितरौ नान्वविन्देतां कृष्णोदारार्भकेहितम् । गायन्त्यद्यापि कवयो यल्लोकशमलापहम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: pitarau nānvavindetāṃ kṛṣṇodārārbhakehitam gāyantyadyāpi kavayo yallokaśamalāpaham

translation: While the (real) parents of Kṛṣṇa could not witness the childly sports of Kṛṣṇa which wipe out the sins of the world and which are still extolled by the wise and learned people.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । द्रोणो वसूनां प्रवरो धरया भार्यया सह । करिष्यमाण आदेशान् ब्रह्मणस्तमुवाच ह ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca droṇo vasūnāṃ pravaro dharayā bhāryayā saha kariṣyamāṇa ādeśān brahmaṇastamuvāca ha

translation:
Śuka replied:
When Droṇa, the foremost of Vasus, along with his wife Dharā was intent upon executing the orders of god Brahmā, he requested that divinity.

devanagari: जातयोर्नौ महादेवे भुवि विश्वेश्वरे हरौ । भक्तिः स्यात्परमा लोके ययाञ्जो दुर्गतिं तरेत् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: jātayornau mahādeve bhuvi viśveśvare harau bhaktiḥ syātparamā loke yayāñjo durgatiṃ taret

translation: “When we are born on the earth, may we entertain the highest devotion to Hari, the Supreme God, and the Lord of the universe—devotion through which a person easily and certainly overcomes evil destiny in this world.”

devanagari: अस्त्वित्युक्तः स भगवान् व्रजे द्रोणो महायशाः । जज्ञे नन्द इति ख्यातो यशोदा सा धराभवत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: astvityuktaḥ sa bhagavān vraje droṇo mahāyaśāḥ jajñe nanda iti khyāto yaśodā sā dharābhavat

translation: ‘Let it be so’, replied the glorious god (granting his prayer). The illustrious Droṇa was born in Vraja and became well-known as Nanda. And the above-mentioned Dharā (his wife) became ‘Yaśodā’.

devanagari: ततो भक्तिर्भगवति पुत्रीभूते जनार्दने । दम्पत्योर्नितरामासीत् गोपगोपीषु भारत ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato bhaktirbhagavati putrībhūte janārdane dampatyornitarāmāsīt gopagopīṣu bhārata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Hence, out of all Gopa men and women, that couple entertained the most intense and passionate devotion to the glorious Lord Viṣṇu (Janārdana) who became their son, Oh descendant of Bharata.

devanagari: कृष्णो ब्रह्मण आदेशं सत्यं कर्तुं व्रजे विभुः । सहरामो वसंश्चक्रे तेषां प्रीतिं स्वलीलया ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇo brahmaṇa ādeśaṃ satyaṃ kartuṃ vraje vibhuḥ saharāmo vasaṃścakre teṣāṃ prītiṃ svalīlayā

translation:
In order to carry out (and fulfil) Brahmā’s boon (to them), the Omnipresent Lord Kṛṣṇa along with Balarāma lived at Vraja and brought delight to them all, by his sportive activities.
